title: The Old Man And The Key

jokes.text: Homer crashes the car during opening sequence - D'oh! (SCREAMS) (BRAKES SCREECHING)
jokes.setup: Classic Simpsons opening sequence car crash
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature 'D'oh!' exclamation
jokes.explanation: Iconic opening sequence moment - not original but perfectly executed character comedy

jokes.text: Not now, Marge. I'm waiting for the new XFL season! / The 'X' is for 'Extreme.'
jokes.setup: Homer enthusiastically waiting for sports season
jokes.punchline: His naive explanation of what XFL stands for
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's sports obsession and simple-mindedness

jokes.text: There is no XFL this year. The league folded. / (EXCLAIMS) Who told you? / Last year's MVP. He sweeps up toenails at the beauty parlor.
jokes.setup: Homer asking who told Marge about XFL folding
jokes.punchline: The MVP now has a gross, demeaning job
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor about failed sports leagues - specific detail about toenails elevates it

jokes.text: Last year's MVP. He sweeps up toenails at the beauty parlor.
jokes.setup: Homer asks who told her about XFL folding
jokes.punchline: Former football star's degrading new job with gross specificity
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly specific and grotesque detail about fallen athlete - good dark humor

jokes.text: Your parent... Abraham Simpson... is dead. / Complications of a medical nature. The nursing home was not responsible.
jokes.punchline: Corporate liability disclaimer in death announcement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of corporate callousness - automated death notice with liability waiver

jokes.text: And he never even lived to be a vegetable.
jokes.punchline: Expressing regret he didn't live to be in vegetative state
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - mourning that his father missed the 'opportunity' of being a vegetable

jokes.text: Sure, I said I loved him, but never that I was in love with him.
jokes.setup: Marge saying Homer was open with his feelings
jokes.punchline: Homer's inappropriate romantic distinction about his father
jokes.explanation: Brilliant uncomfortable comedy - Homer confusing familial and romantic love in the worst context

jokes.text: What's got into you punks? / The home told us you were dead. / Me? I ain't dead. It was Stamson down the hall.
jokes.setup: Family thinking Abe is dead and grieving
jokes.punchline: Casual reveal it was wrong person who died
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection reveal but somewhat predictable given the setup

jokes.text: So much for Mr. I Can Button My Own Shirt.
jokes.punchline: Mocking his independence claims after death
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about nursing home independence - specific detail makes it memorable

jokes.text: Yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah. We'll call you or send you some fruit.
jokes.setup: Homer offering second chance at relationship
jokes.punchline: Abe's immediate dismissal with generic promises
jokes.explanation: Typical Abe dismissiveness - deflates sentimental moment effectively

jokes.text: Hi. I'm Zelda. I put the 'ass' in assisted living.
jokes.punchline: Sexual wordplay on 'assisted living'
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay establishing character as sexually forward elderly woman

jokes.text: Abe Simpson's the name, and now that you're here, I'm changing my instructions to 'do resuscitate.'
jokes.setup: Abe introducing himself to attractive Zelda
jokes.punchline: Reversing DNR order because of romantic interest
jokes.explanation: Clever play on medical directives as pickup line - perfectly aged character humor

jokes.text: Hey, who's the tall drink of Maalox?
jokes.setup: Elderly woman commenting on attractive man
jokes.punchline: Substituting antacid for water in classic phrase
jokes.explanation: Age-appropriate twist on classic expression - fits the setting well

jokes.text: Eh, that's Zack. He's as smooth as a 50-year-old behind.
jokes.punchline: Age-adjusted version of smooth compliment
jokes.explanation: Good age-appropriate humor adjusting expectations for elderly characters

jokes.text: It's got a wheelchair lift. (ALL EXCLAIM) (ALL BREATHING HEAVILY)
jokes.punchline: Women getting excited about accessibility feature
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion - wheelchair lift as sexy feature for elderly women

jokes.text: Is this the Dairy Queen? (MOANING)
jokes.setup: Abe after his license was revoked - flashback to crash
jokes.punchline: Confused elderly driver after accident
jokes.explanation: Standard elderly driver confusion joke - works but not particularly original

jokes.text: But Zelda, if you're looking for something with a big backseat and a lot of gas, I'm your man.
jokes.setup: Abe trying to impress Zelda about his car
jokes.punchline: Double entendre about his bodily functions
jokes.explanation: Age-appropriate innuendo that works on multiple levels for elderly character

jokes.text: You've never heard Garrison Keillor till you've heard him through five speakers.
jokes.setup: Abe bragging about his car's audio system
jokes.punchline: Using high-end audio for most boring radio host
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy - elderly man's idea of impressive audio experience

jokes.text: You sunk my Scrabbleship. / This game makes no sense. / Tell that to the good men who just lost their lives. Semper Fi.
jokes.punchline: Abe treating word game casualties as military honor
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation from board games to military honor - perfectly absurd Simpsons humor

jokes.text: That was a DWI!
jokes.punchline: Homer correcting to less serious charge
jokes.explanation: Simple character moment showing Homer's priorities - functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: Statistics show that old people drive at least as well as sleep-deprived apes.
jokes.punchline: Comparing them to sleep-deprived apes as positive
jokes.explanation: Great backhanded defense - comparing elderly drivers to apes as a positive argument

jokes.text: Dad, get up. / No, I'm dead. / Dead! / Dead, dead, dead, dead, dead!
jokes.punchline: Escalating fake death tantrum
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of childish elderly behavior - repetition builds the comedy

jokes.text: Woo-hoo! And I want driving gloves. / No way! / Dead, dead, dead, dead!
jokes.setup: Abe immediately pushing for more after getting license agreement
jokes.punchline: Return to death threat over gloves
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Abe's manipulation tactics - immediate return to threats

jokes.text: Leo, slow down. That looks like a nice place to eat. / Carla's daughter works in that building.
jokes.punchline: Treating simulation as real leisurely drive
jokes.explanation: Good elderly driving humor - treating simulation as social outing

jokes.text: You know, it's kind of ironic. These old people are being kept alive by the organs of the young people they ran over.
jokes.punchline: Dark observation about elderly drivers and organ donation cycle
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor about the ironic cycle of elderly driving accidents and medical care

jokes.text: That's my seeing eye. The right one's my winking eye.
jokes.punchline: Abe's illogical eye designations
jokes.explanation: Good absurd logic from Abe - treating eyes as having specialized functions

jokes.text: I'll give you your license if you never do that again. / Oh, everything's the last time I do everything.
jokes.punchline: Abe's melancholy about aging and mortality
jokes.explanation: Touching and funny observation about aging - balances humor with pathos

jokes.text: Oh, no. I'm not giving you my car. You'd probably drive it.
jokes.setup: Abe asking to borrow the car
jokes.punchline: Homer's 'concern' about car being used for driving
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - stating the obvious purpose as a negative

jokes.text: Wow, a booth. If I'm tired, I can just lie down.
jokes.punchline: Abe's excitement about booth's sleeping potential
jokes.explanation: Good elderly character humor - simple pleasures and practical thinking

jokes.text: This is the best party of my life. / Bro, you tapped the septic tank.
jokes.punchline: Revelation about sewage accident
jokes.explanation: Standard gross-out humor - functional but relies on shock value

jokes.text: Movies were better in our day. For a nickel you got two movies, a cartoon, a bag of popcorn and a whipping. Kept your mind on your business.
jokes.punchline: Including corporal punishment in entertainment package
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of 'good old days' nostalgia - builds to absurd inclusion of violence

jokes.text: Save your breath. Seriously. / Well, I gotta do something before the Viagra kicks in.
jokes.setup: Zelda telling Abe to save his breath
jokes.punchline: Abe's TMI about medication timing
jokes.explanation: Age-appropriate cringe humor - Abe oversharing about medication

jokes.text: Hey, I've earned their trust. / Oh! Everybody trusts Lisa. Precious little Lisa. Apple of her daddy's eye.
jokes.setup: Lisa defending her freedom compared to Abe
jokes.punchline: Abe's jealous mimicking of family favoritism
jokes.explanation: Good jealousy humor showing family dynamics - Abe's petty resentment

jokes.text: She's no hoochie. Her name is Zelda and she understands me. / Grampa, I gotta tell you, she's a stone-cold hoochie. / Straight up, Marge. That hoochie only likes you because you can drive.
jokes.setup: Abe defending Zelda against hoochie accusations
jokes.punchline: Family escalating hoochie references while analyzing the relationship
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of word repetition with family ganging up on Abe's denial

jokes.text: He is your dad. / Cosmic.
jokes.setup: Marge pointing out Abe is Homer's father
jokes.punchline: Homer's amazed reaction to obvious fact
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer stupidity - treating basic family relationship as profound revelation

jokes.text: I'll take one, Achu. / No, not 'chu,' 'pu.' / I got troubles with both.
jokes.punchline: Turning correction into health complaint
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay turning name pronunciation into bodily function jokes

jokes.text: Finally the shakes are working for me.
jokes.punchline: Finding positive use for hand tremors
jokes.explanation: Great elderly humor - finding silver lining in medical condition

jokes.text: Your scrapings have landed on my jacket from Planet Hollywood, Orlando.
jokes.setup: Tough guy confronting Abe about lottery scrapings
jokes.punchline: Undermining toughness with tourist jacket specificity
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of tough guy image with touristy jacket detail

jokes.text: I should cut you like I cut sodium out of my diet.
jokes.punchline: Comparing violence to dietary restrictions
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection threat - elderly health concerns undermining tough talk

jokes.text: Los Souvenir Jacquitos!
jokes.punchline: Gang named after tourist merchandise
jokes.explanation: Creative gang name subverting expectations with tourist theme

jokes.text: Some sort of death race? / That's what I would do.
jokes.setup: Abe suggesting death race as conflict resolution
jokes.punchline: Casual endorsement of extreme solution
jokes.explanation: Good absurd escalation with casual endorsement of violence

jokes.text: What are you scared of, old man? / Everything. Dogs, Dutchmen, the gathering darkness.
jokes.punchline: Abe's honest and escalating list of fears
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from mundane to existential fears - great character honesty

jokes.text: When I yank down my girlfriend's tube top, the race will begin. / What? I'm just trying to bring you into my world.
jokes.punchline: Defensive explanation about sharing his lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Moderately funny cringe humor about inappropriate behavior

jokes.text: I would rather die than have people not know what stores I have been to.
jokes.punchline: Prioritizing tourist documentation over safety
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd priorities - dying for tourist jacket bragging rights

jokes.text: We're the baddest punks in our age bracket!
jokes.setup: Abe celebrating victory in death race
jokes.punchline: Qualifying toughness by age group
jokes.explanation: Great age-appropriate boasting - limiting tough guy claims to demographic

jokes.text: My beard. I broke my beard.
jokes.punchline: Treating beard like breakable object
jokes.explanation: Decent absurd injury complaint - treating facial hair like equipment

jokes.text: I've booked us a room for the weekend in Branson, Missouri. The Gritz Carlton Hotel.
jokes.punchline: Pun on Ritz Carlton with 'grits'
jokes.explanation: Solid pun on luxury hotel name fitting Branson demographic

jokes.text: I'll always think of you as my friend without a car.
jokes.punchline: Reducing him to his transportation status
jokes.explanation: Good put-down disguised as sentiment - reducing person to utility

jokes.text: Zelda, I'm gonna make you forget about your grandchildren.
jokes.punchline: Age-inappropriate pickup line about grandchildren
jokes.explanation: Age-appropriate version of typical pickup line - moderately funny

jokes.text: And if I go to prison, I'll get better food and more hugs.
jokes.punchline: Prison as upgrade from nursing home
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about nursing home conditions vs. prison

jokes.text: It's The Itchy and Scratchy Hour, presented by Hansen's Moustache Wax, the moustache wax Hitler doesn't use.
jokes.punchline: WWII-era Hitler reference as negative endorsement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant period-appropriate advertising using Hitler as negative endorsement

jokes.text: During the war, Eleanor Roosevelt was the voice of Scratchy. / The lady knows funny.
jokes.setup: Historical trivia about cartoon voice acting
jokes.punchline: Bart's respectful assessment of First Lady's comedy skills
jokes.explanation: Good absurd historical reference with Bart's casual acceptance

jokes.text: Oh, sure, when he does something bad, he's my father.
jokes.punchline: Homer noting selective family ownership
jokes.explanation: Relatable family humor about claiming/disowning relatives based on behavior

jokes.text: These are hobo signs. They're to tell other hobos if a place is friendly or not. That one means good vittles. Sexy daughter. Mass hobo grave in cellar.
jokes.punchline: Escalating from practical to sexual to horrifying information
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation in hobo warning signs from benign to disturbing

jokes.text: No, pally. This is Bronson, Missouri. / Well, how do we get to Branson? / Number 10 bus.
jokes.punchline: Wrong town with similar name and casual directions
jokes.explanation: Basic misdirection humor with similar town names - functional but not inspired

jokes.text: Hey, Ma, how about some cookies? / No dice. / This ain't over.
jokes.punchline: Treating minor rejection as serious conflict
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of minor domestic dispute into dramatic threat

jokes.text: Was it drugs or a car crash Or facelift gone wrong? No, they're right here in Branson And singing this song
jokes.punchline: Dark speculation about celebrity deaths vs. Branson careers
jokes.explanation: Good meta commentary on celebrity career trajectories and mortality

jokes.text: Tennessee Ernie Ford? Now, I know you're dead. / No. You just think I'm dead. / No, you're dead. I was your biggest fan. Look, I clipped your obituary.
jokes.punchline: Argument over celebrity's death status with obituary as proof
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd argument about celebrity death with fan producing obituary

jokes.text: Oh, no, there's gambling. We just have to find it! / Look, Mom, it's your car. / Bet you they're not. Three to one. How much you got? / Mom, you're hurting me.
jokes.setup: Lisa saying there's no gambling in Branson
jokes.punchline: Marge immediately proving her wrong and getting violent about betting
jokes.explanation: Good character subversion showing Marge's hidden gambling problem emerging

jokes.text: It's Captain Bringdown and The Buzzkillers.
jokes.punchline: Bart's band name for family as party-enders
jokes.explanation: Good Bart nickname for family - captures dynamic of adults ending fun

jokes.text: Nobody dumps Abe Simpson. You're nothing but a hoochie. / (CHANTING) Hoochie, hoochie, hoochie!
jokes.punchline: Audience joining in the insult chant
jokes.explanation: Good callback to family hoochie discussion with public humiliation

jokes.text: Son, can you forgive an old fool? / Only in public.
jokes.punchline: Homer's conditional forgiveness based on setting
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's shallow but practical approach to family

jokes.text: In Russia, stage is for performers only.
jokes.punchline: Russian performer's stern rule enforcement
jokes.explanation: Basic foreign character humor with authority enforcement - functional but standard

tmdb_overview: Grampa falls in love with Zelda, a woman who has an interest in men who can drive. He decides to get his driver's license back, but is ignorant to Homer and Marge's concerns that she is only using him for his car.
